text:
Deep learning (DL) has emerged as a transformative force in medical image analysis, offering the ability to process vast and complex healthcare datasets in real time. By automating feature extraction and identifying intricate patterns, DL algorithms significantly enhance diagnostic accuracy, operational efficiency, and clinical outcomes. This paper provides a comprehensive review of contemporary deep learning methodologies—including Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) units, and hybrid architectures—tailored for medical applications.

Through a systematic literature review, we categorize existing research into five technical domains, evaluating each based on critical performance metrics such as accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, robustness, and computational complexity. Our findings highlight a surge in high-impact research particularly around 2021, with Python serving as the primary implementation language across the field. We further examine practical case studies ranging from skin cancer detection to tumor segmentation, while addressing persistent hurdles such as data scarcity, interpretability, and security concerns. By identifying current gaps in multi-modal analysis and federated learning, this study serves as a foundational roadmap for future advancements in AI-driven medical imaging and public health informatics.
